doc: V1.3 Toy 2 — the Chord player (ADR-0022). Seven buttons play the diatonic triads I–vii° of the current key: tap-and-hold sustains a chord, release stops it. Each button sends /chord/set [degree, gate] (a chord-root scale degree + gate); the `chord` op stacks scale-relative thirds and emits `degree` notes, which the voicer resolves through the tonal context — so held and tapped chords RE-SPELL LIVE when you change the key (send /context/root [48..72]). A pad voice (saw -> filter -> slow-attack envelope) and a 'brightness' Good Button (/brightness/in [0..1] -> filter cutoff) round it out. Polyphonic: 12 voices cover four held seventh chords. Built entirely from composition — no new instrument-format machinery.

nodes.doc: Tap-to-harmony: /chord/set [degree, gate] -> stacked-thirds `degree` notes. `size` 3 = triad (default), 4 = seventh. No context input — the voicer resolves degrees, so chords re-spell on key change. The 7 chord buttons each send a fixed root degree 0..6 (I–vii°) via the chord-button widget.
The tonal context the voicer resolves degrees against. KEY SELECTOR: send /context/root [48..72] (MIDI tonic) to transpose; held & tapped chords re-spell live. Default C major.
Polyphonic note allocator. Degree notes from /chord re-spell through /context. 12 voices = four held seventh chords.
Saw pad tone (harmonics for the filter to shape).
Lowpass; cutoff is a Signal input driven by the brightness Good Button.
Slow-attack pad envelope (linear CV), gated by the voicer.
Shape the linear envelope CV into an exponential-style volume curve (x^2).

Per-voice headroom: constant equal-power gain (1/sqrt(12) ~ 0.289) held forever (no input). Single-lane -> broadcasts to every voice's /vmix. The 12 voice lanes sum straight into the master (no auto gain comp), so each voice is scaled here to keep a full 12-voice chord off the clip ceiling while staying loud at a typical 3-6 note chord.
Per-voice gain stage: env audio (a) * /vgain scalar (b). Replicated per voice (inherits the 12 lanes from env); the constant gain broadcasts in on b.
PUBLIC Good Button. Identity [0,1] pass-through, fanned to the ranged cutoff map. Send /brightness/in [0..1]. Rests at 0.5 (ADR-0018).
Smooth the mapped cutoff into a CV signal; default 1800 Hz before any knob move.
